    1. mapRow(ResultSet, int)

Class ReferentialPayloadRowMapper<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.store.mapper.ReferentialPayloadRowMapper<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the business payload
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.springframework.jdbc.core.RowMapper<ReferentialPayload<T>>
public class ReferentialPayloadRowMapper<T> extends Object implements org.springframework.jdbc.core.RowMapper<ReferentialPayload<T>>
Spring JDBC RowMapper that converts a row from the FAILOVER_STORE table into a ReferentialPayload.

Column mapping:

  • FAILOVER_NAME / FAILOVER_KEY — identity fields
  • AS_OF / EXPIRE_ON — temporal metadata (required; a NULL value in either column triggers a FailoverStoreException)
  • PAYLOAD_CLASS — fully-qualified class name resolved via Serializer.toClass(String)
  • PAYLOAD — serialized payload extracted by the PayloadColumnResolver and deserialized via Serializer.deserialize(String, Class)

The upToDate flag is always set to false for rows read from the store.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Method Details

    • mapRow

      public ReferentialPayload<T> mapRow(ResultSet rs, int rowNum) throws SQLException
      Maps the current ResultSet row to a ReferentialPayload.
      Specified by:
      mapRow in interface org.springframework.jdbc.core.RowMapper<T>
      Parameters:
      rs - the ResultSet positioned on the current row
      rowNum - the row number (unused)
      Returns:
      the mapped payload with upToDate set to false
      Throws:
      SQLException - if any column cannot be read
      FailoverStoreException - if AS_OF or EXPIRE_ON is NULL, indicating a corrupt row
